title: anthriscus caucalis var. neglecta
intro: Anthriscus caucalis var. neglecta, commonly known as wild chervil or keck, is a plant species in the carrot family that has been used for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its carminative and antispasmodic properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues such as bloating and cramps. In herbal medicine, Anthriscus caucalis var. neglecta is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and acting as an antiseptic. It can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used as a flavoring agent in food and beverages.
